THE MATTER IS REMANDED TO RESPONDENT FOR CONSIDERATION OF THE ADDITIONAL EVIDENCE BY PETITIONER PRIOR TO RESPONDENT'S RE HEARING. ADMINISTRATIVE RECORDS RETURNED TO PETITIONER IN OPEN COURT. LAW AND MOTION 301, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITIONER CENTER FOR BIOLOGICAL DIVERSITY'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES IS CONTINUED TO 6/21/11 PER AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)
MINI-MINUTES FOR MAY-26-2011 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 301
REPLY IN SUPPORT OF PETITIONERS MOTION FOR ATTY'S FEES FILED BY PETITIONER CENTER FOR BIOLOGICAL DIVERSITY, A NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATION
MINI-MINUTES FOR JUN-21-2011 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 301

LAW AND MOTION 301, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITIONER CENTER FOR BIOLOGICAL DIVERSITY'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES IS DENIED. VIEWED PRACTICALLY, PLAINTIFF'S SUCCESS IN OBTAINING FURTHER REVIEW, BASED ON ITS SUPPLEMENTAL SUBMISSION, AFTER THE PRIOR REMAND, HAS NOT CONFERRED A SUBSTANTIAL PUBLIC BENEFIT GIVEN THAT PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ACIEVED ITS PRIMARY OBJECTIVE OF FORCING RESPONDENT TO TREAT THE PIKA AS A CANDIDATE SPECIES AND THAT PLAINTIFF HAD ALTERNATIVE ADMINISTRATIVE AVENUES TO MAKE SURE ITS ADDITIONAL INFORMATION WOULD BE CONSIDERED. OPPOSING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER, CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Attorneys' Fees
Off Calendar Jun-21-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
PETITIONER CENTER FOR BIOLOGICAL DIVERSITY'S MOTION For Attorneys' Fees IS DENIED. VIEWED PRACTICALLY, PLAINTIFF'S SUCCESS IN OBTAINING FURTHER REVIEW, BASED ON ITS SUPPLEMENTAL SUBMISSION, AFTER THE PRIOR REMAND, HAS NOT CONFERRED A SUBSTANTIAL PUBLIC BENEFIT GIVEN THAT PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ACIEVED ITS PRIMARY OBJECTIVE OF FORCING RESPONDENT TO TREAT THE PIKA AS A CANDIDATE SPECIES AND THAT PLAINTIFF HAD ALTERNATIVE ADMINISTRATIVE AVENUES TO MAKE SURE ITS ADDITIONAL INFORMATION WOULD BE CONSIDERED. Opposing party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter, Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 301/PJB

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Attorneys' Fees
Off Calendar May-26-2011 Continued To Jun-21-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
PETITIONER CENTER FOR BIOLOGICAL DIVERSITY'S MOTION For Attorneys' Fees IS CONTINUED TO 6/21/11 PER AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =(301/PJB)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Oct-19-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Gregory C. Loarie, Esq. Earth Justice, 510-550-6725, For Petitioner and moving party Center for Biological Diversity
Daniel S. Harris, Esq., and Cecile Dennis, Esq. Attorney General's Office, 415-703-5530, for respondent and opposing party California Fish and Game Commission

ARGUED and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PETITIONER CENTER FOR BIOLOGICAL DIVERSITY's Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate is Granted. The matter is remanded to respondent for consideration of the additional evidence by Petitioner prior to respondent's re hearing.
Administrative records returned to petitioner in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 (301/PJB)
